findings.title: Skeleton invisible on ticker-change when React Query has stale cached data — stale bars flash instead
findings.suggested_fix: Conditional chain: ohlcv.length > 0 renders Chart; skeleton only shows when ohlcv.length==0 AND ohlcvLoading==true. React Query isLoading is true only when isPending (no cached data exists). When user switches ticker but a prior fetch for that ticker is in React Querys 5-minute stale cache, isLoading stays false and the cached ohlcv for the OLD ticker populates ohlcv (EMPTY_OHLCV default never fires because useQuery returns cached value). Chart renders with prior tickers bars during background refetch; skeleton never displays. Fix: use isFetching instead of isLoading, or add placeholderData: keepPreviousData(undefined) so ohlcv resets on queryKey change.

findings.title: Startup cascade: new validator rejects bots.json with pre-PR logic values, BotManager.load() silently swallows all bots
findings.suggested_fix: BotConfig(**cfg_dict) at line 597 now runs validate_logic. If any persisted bot has a non-uppercase logic value (possible from any client that sent lowercase before this PR, or from manual JSON edit), validator raises ValueError. The outer try/except at lines 583-605 catches all exceptions with logger.exception() and returns silently, leaving self.bots empty. Server starts with zero bots and no user-visible error. Fix: normalize before checking — change the validator body to v = v.upper(); if v not in (AND, OR) in all five validate_logic implementations. This mirrors the ticker validator which does v.strip().upper() before checking.

findings.title: detail?.state.last_tick in new statusTooltip IIFE: non-optional chain on .state throws TypeError if detail is truthy with undefined state
findings.suggested_fix: New IIFE at line 127 uses detail?.state.last_tick. Optional chaining stops at detail but .state is accessed unconditionally if detail is truthy. If a backend response returns {config:..., state:undefined} during a race between bot creation and first detail poll, detail is truthy but detail.state is undefined, throwing TypeError and crashing BotCard render. The same pattern exists pre-PR at lines 44, 170, 295, 302, 337 (systemic pre-existing pattern the PR replicates). Fix: use detail?.state?.last_tick throughout. Marking pre_existing=true because the pattern predates this PR.

findings.title: cache_info 6-tuple unpack restores correctness but remains positionally brittle — next cache key dimension crashes /api/cache again
findings.suggested_fix: F28c correctly changes 5-tuple unpack to 6-tuple. But _evict_cache at line 410 still accesses k[2] and k[3] by index, and cache_info uses positional destructuring. Both crash with ValueError or silently use wrong field if key grows. Consider a typed CacheKey NamedTuple so references become k.end, k.interval rather than k[2], k[3]. The _ext wildcard also discards extended_hours from the diagnostic output, making /api/cache incomplete for debugging extended-hours cache state.

findings.title: NaN guard in OptimizerPanel shows misleading error when system-computed defaultMin/defaultMax is NaN rather than user input
findings.suggested_fix: Guard fires when p.min is empty so minN = opt.defaultMin. If opt.defaultMin is NaN (e.g., trailing_stop.value is undefined making Math.max(0.1, undefined * 0.3) = NaN in paramOptions.ts line 27), user sees the error even though they never typed anything. The guard correctly prevents NaN from reaching backend but blames the user for a system-computed value. Fix: in paramOptions.ts add isFinite() guards on all computed defaultMin/defaultMax, or distinguish user-entered vs default-value NaN in the error message.

residual_risks: validate_logic does not normalize case. Any persisted bots.json with lowercase logic values will trigger silent startup failure on next deploy (see P1 finding). Even if bots.json currently contains only uppercase values, no migration guard prevents a future lowercase write.
_ext wildcard in cache_info discards extended_hours from diagnostic output. /api/cache cannot distinguish an extended-hours cache entry from a regular-hours entry for the same ticker/interval/dates.
Skeleton feature does not cover the stale-data refetch path (Finding 1) — during 5-minute stale window on ticker change, user sees wrong data rather than skeleton, undermining the UX intent of A14.
testing_gaps: No test for BotManager.load() when bots.json contains buy_logic lowercase value — the startup silent-swallow is the highest-severity failure mode introduced by this PR.
No test for skeleton display when useOHLCV has a stale cache hit on ticker change — React Query isLoading vs isFetching semantics are the crux of Finding 1.
No test for UpdateBotRequest with {buy_logic: null} to document whether null is accepted-and-ignored or should produce 422.
